news.title: Why Was the Market Down Today?
news.summary: Related-company context for GOOGL via MSFT: Several software companies affected by disappointing Microsoft earnings last week, including Atlassian, Datadog and Intuit, fell at least 7%.  Other big tech stocks from Nvidia to Broadcom and Oracle struggled.  Not all tech stocks dropped, however.

news.title: Looking At The Apple AAPL Narrative After Gemini Deal And Margin Concerns
news.summary: The latest update on Apple’s stock story includes a small lift in fair value, from US$286.58 to US$291.65 per share, alongside a slightly higher discount rate at 8.31% and more conservative long run revenue growth assumptions at 6.99%. Those tweaks sit against a backdrop of ongoing debate about the pace of services growth and how the Google Gemini partnership and memory costs could shape Apple’s financial profile over time. news.title: Meta Platforms (META) Is Up 5.1% After Unveiling Massive 2026 AI Capex Plan And ESOP Shelf Registration – Has The Bull Case Changed?
news.summary: Related-company context for GOOGL via META: In late January 2026, Meta Platforms reported fourth-quarter 2025 revenue of US$59.89 billion and full-year revenue of US$200.97 billion, alongside guidance for first-quarter 2026 revenue of US$53.5 billion to US$56.5 billion and a massive planned 2026 capital expenditure increase to US$115 billion–US$135 billion focused on artificial intelligence infrastructure. news.title: US files appeal in Google search case, court filing shows
news.summary: government ​and a majority ‌of U. states on ‌Tuesday said they will appeal the outcome of a landmark ⁠antitrust case ‌against Alphabet's Google, according to ‍court papers.

news.title: US files appeal in Google search antitrust case
news.summary: The U.S. government and a majority of U.S. states on Tuesday will appeal the outcome of ​a landmark antitrust case against Alphabet's Google, according to court ‌papers.  A federal court judge in Washington in 2024 ruled Google has a monopoly in ‌the online search business, but rejected the toughest remedies.  The Department of Justice and state attorneys general did not provide details in court documents about their appeal.
